title: Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani

seo.title: Classic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Master the classic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) with our easy, authentic recipe. Get creamy, rich flavors at home. Perfect for any meal!
lowCompetitionKeywords: restaurant style butter chicken at home
h2Headings: How to Make Classic Butter Chicken: Step-by-Step
Pro Tips for the Best Creamy Butter Chicken
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of classic creamy Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) garnished with cilantro and served with naan bread.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich, creamy flavors of our classic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) recipe, perfect for an authentic Indian meal at home.

description: Indulge in the rich, creamy flavors of Butter Chicken, a world-renowned Indian delicacy. This classic Murgh Makhani recipe features tender chicken pieces simmered in a luscious tomato-based gravy, enriched with butter and cream. It is the quintessential comfort food, perfect for a heartwarming meal with family and friends.

ingredients.notes: soaked in warm water for 15 minutes

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine chicken pieces with yogurt, 1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste, 1/2 teaspoon red chili powder, 1/4 teaspoon turmeric, 1/2 teaspoon garam masala, 1/2 teaspoon salt, and lemon juice. Mix well to coat the chicken. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or preferably 2-4 hours.

instructions.title: Cook the Marinated Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 1 tablespoon butter and 1 tablespoon cooking oil in a large pan or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces and cook until browned on all sides and mostly cooked through, about 7-10 minutes. Remove the chicken from the pan and set aside, leaving any drippings in the pan.

instructions.title: Prepare the Sauce Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, add 1 tablespoon butter. Once melted, add the finely chopped onion and sauté until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the remaining 1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste and cook for 1 minute until fragrant. Stir in 1/2 teaspoon red chili powder, 1/4 teaspoon turmeric, 1 teaspoon cumin powder, and 1 teaspoon coriander powder. Cook for 30 seconds.

instructions.title: Simmer and Blend the Sauce
instructions.text: Add the crushed tomatoes, soaked cashews (drained), and remaining 1/2 teaspoon salt to the pan. Bring to a simmer, then reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 10-15 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld. Remove from heat and let it cool slightly. Carefully transfer the mixture to a blender and blend until completely smooth. You may need to add a splash of water if it is too thick.

instructions.title: Combine Chicken and Sauce
instructions.text: Pour the blended sauce back into the pan. Return the cooked chicken pieces to the sauce. Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer over medium-low heat. Cook for another 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, to allow the chicken to finish cooking and absorb the flavors of the sauce.

instructions.title: Finish with Cream and Butter
instructions.text: Stir in the remaining 2 tablespoons of butter, 1/2 cup heavy cream, and the remaining 1/2 teaspoon garam masala. Crush the kasoori methi between your palms and add it to the sauce. Cook for 2-3 minutes, just until heated through and the sauce is rich and creamy. Do not boil after adding cream.

instructions.title: Serve
instructions.text: Garnish with fresh chopped cilantro. Serve hot with naan bread, roti, or basmati rice. Enjoy your authentic classic Butter Chicken!

tips: For the most tender and flavorful chicken, marinate it for at least 2-4 hours, or even overnight in the refrigerator. This allows the spices and yogurt to penetrate deeply.
Ensure your sauce is perfectly smooth by blending it thoroughly. For an extra velvety texture, you can pass the blended sauce through a fine-mesh sieve before returning it to the pan.
Do not skip the Kasoori Methi (dried fenugreek leaves). Crushing them between your palms before adding releases their distinct aromatic flavor, which is quintessential to authentic Butter Chicken.
article:
Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani, is more than just a dish; it's a culinary icon, a global ambassador of Indian cuisine that has captivated palates worldwide. Its rich, creamy, and subtly sweet tomato-based sauce, enveloping succulent pieces of tender chicken, offers a comforting warmth and an explosion of balanced flavors. Whether you're a seasoned chef or a home cook eager to explore Indian recipes, mastering classic Butter Chicken is a rewarding journey that promises delicious results. This article will guide you through the heritage, ingredients, and step-by-step process to create this beloved dish in your own kitchen.

## What is Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani)?

Murgh Makhani, literally translating to "chicken with butter," is a quintessential Indian curry originating from Delhi in the 1950s. It was reportedly created by Kundan Lal Jaggi, one of the founders of Moti Mahal restaurant, who experimented by mixing leftover tandoori chicken in a rich tomato and butter gravy. The result was a revelation: a mild, tangy, and incredibly creamy dish that quickly became a sensation.

What sets Butter Chicken apart is its unique flavor profile – a delicate balance of sweet, savory, and mildly spicy notes, achieved through a blend of aromatic spices, ripe tomatoes, generous amounts of butter, and a touch of cream. Unlike many other Indian curries that can be quite fiery, Butter Chicken is known for its approachable mildness, making it a favorite for those new to Indian food and a beloved staple for connoisseurs. The chicken is typically marinated in yogurt and spices before being cooked, contributing to its tenderness and depth of flavor.

## Ingredients for Authentic Butter Chicken

Crafting authentic Butter Chicken requires a careful selection of fresh ingredients and a blend of specific spices. Here's what you'll need:

For the Chicken Marinade:
*   **Chicken:** 1.5 lbs boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breast, cut into 1-inch pieces. Thighs are preferred for their tenderness.
*   **Plain Yogurt:** 1/2 cup, for tenderizing and flavor.
*   **Ginger-Garlic Paste:** 1 tablespoon.
*   **Spices:** 1 teaspoon red chili powder (Kashmiri for color), 1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder, 1 teaspoon garam masala, salt to taste.

For the Makhani (Butter) Sauce:
*   **Butter:** 4-6 tablespoons (unsalted), crucial for the richness.
*   **Oil:** 1 tablespoon vegetable or ghee.
*   **Onion:** 1 medium, finely chopped.
*   **Ginger-Garlic Paste:** 1 tablespoon.
*   **Tomatoes:** 1 (28-ounce) can crushed tomatoes or 4-5 large ripe fresh tomatoes, pureed.
*   **Cashews:** 1/4 cup, soaked in warm water for 15 minutes, then pureed (for thickness and richness).
*   **Spices:** 1 teaspoon red chili powder (Kashmiri), 1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder, 1 teaspoon garam masala, 1 teaspoon sugar (to balance acidity).
*   **Kasuri Methi (Dried Fenugreek Leaves):** 1-2 teaspoons, lightly crushed. This is a non-negotiable ingredient for the authentic flavor.
*   **Heavy Cream:** 1/2 cup, for the characteristic creaminess.
*   **Salt:** To taste.
*   **Fresh Cilantro:** For garnish.

## How to Make Classic Butter Chicken: Step-by-Step

Follow these steps to create a show-stopping Butter Chicken:

1.  **Marinate the Chicken:** In a large bowl, combine chicken pieces with yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, red chili powder, turmeric, garam masala, and salt. Mix well to coat. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or ideally 2-4 hours, or even overnight for maximum flavor.

2.  **Cook the Chicken:** Heat 1 tablespoon of oil in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, working in batches if necessary to avoid overcrowding. Cook until lightly browned and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes per side. Set aside.

3.  **Prepare the Sauce Base:** In the same pan, melt 2 tablespoons of butter over medium heat. Add chopped onions and sauté until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until fragrant.

4.  **Add Tomatoes and Spices:** Stir in the crushed tomatoes (or fresh puree), red chili powder, turmeric, garam masala, and sugar. Bring to a simmer, then add the pureed cashews. Cover and cook for 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce thickens and the oil starts to separate from the edges.

5.  **Blend the Sauce:** Carefully transfer the cooked sauce mixture to a blender. Blend until completely smooth. For an extra velvety texture, you can strain the blended sauce through a fine-mesh sieve back into the pan. This step is key for that restaurant-quality smoothness of Butter Chicken.

6.  **Finish the Curry:** Return the smooth sauce to the pan. Add the remaining 2-4 tablespoons of butter, kasuri methi (crushed between your palms to release its aroma), and the cooked chicken pieces. Stir gently to combine.

7.  **Add Cream and Simmer:** Pour in the heavy cream and stir well. Bring the curry to a gentle simmer (do not boil rapidly, as cream can curdle). Taste and adjust salt if needed. Simmer for 5-10 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the chicken to absorb the sauce.

8.  **Garnish and Serve:** Garnish with fresh cilantro before serving.

## Pro Tips for the Best Creamy Butter Chicken

Achieving that perfect, rich, and creamy Butter Chicken is an art. Here are some pro tips:

*   **Chicken Thighs are Key:** While chicken breast can be used, boneless, skinless chicken thighs stay much more tender and moist in the rich sauce, resisting drying out.
*   **Marination Time:** Don't rush it. The longer the chicken marinates, the more tender and flavorful it will be. Overnight is ideal.
*   **Quality Tomatoes:** Use good quality canned crushed tomatoes or very ripe fresh tomatoes. They form the backbone of your sauce.
*   **Blending and Straining:** For an ultra-smooth, restaurant-style sauce, blending the cooked sauce base until completely smooth and then straining it through a fine-mesh sieve is highly recommended.
*   **Don't Skip Kasuri Methi:** Dried fenugreek leaves are crucial for the authentic flavor profile of Murgh Makhani. Lightly toast them in a dry pan for a minute before crushing and adding to enhance their aroma.
*   **Balance the Flavors:** The sugar helps balance the acidity of the tomatoes. Taste and adjust salt, sugar, and chili to your preference.
*   **Gentle Simmer:** When adding cream, ensure the curry is at a gentle simmer, not a rolling boil, to prevent the cream from curdling.

## Serving Suggestions & Pairings

Butter Chicken is a versatile dish that pairs beautifully with a variety of accompaniments. For the ultimate Indian dining experience, consider these pairings:

*   **Breads:** Warm, fluffy naan bread (garlic naan is a popular choice), tandoori roti, or paratha are perfect for scooping up the rich sauce.
*   **Rice:** Steamed basmati rice or fragrant jeera rice (cumin rice) provides a lovely contrast and helps soak up the delicious gravy.
*   **Cooling Sides:** A simple cucumber raita (yogurt with grated cucumber and spices) or a fresh green salad can offer a refreshing counterpoint to the richness of the curry.
*   **Condiments:** Mango chutney or a simple onion salad can add extra layers of flavor and texture.
*   **Beverages:** A tall glass of cooling mango lassi or a crisp Indian lager complements the meal wonderfully.

## Variations and FAQs

Butter Chicken is beloved, and naturally, there are ways to adapt it and common questions that arise.

**Variations:**
*   **Healthier Version:** Reduce the amount of butter and cream, or use low-fat cream. You can also opt for lean chicken breast.
*   **Vegetarian/Vegan:** Replace chicken with paneer (Indian cheese), tofu, or mixed vegetables for Paneer Makhani or Tofu Makhani. Use coconut cream and oil instead of dairy butter and cream for a vegan version.
*   **Smoky Flavor:** For an authentic smoky touch, char the cooked chicken pieces slightly under a broiler or grill before adding to the sauce. Some even use a charcoal "dhungar" method where a piece of hot charcoal is placed in the curry with a drizzle of ghee, covered, to infuse smoke.

**FAQs:**
*   **Is Butter Chicken spicy?** Traditionally, Butter Chicken is mild to medium in spice level. The heat comes from red chili powder, which can be adjusted to your preference. Kashmiri red chili powder is often used for its vibrant color rather than intense heat.
*   **What's the difference between Butter Chicken and Chicken Tikka Masala?** While similar, there are key differences. Butter Chicken is generally sweeter, creamier, and milder, with the chicken often cooked in the sauce itself after a light pan-fry. Chicken Tikka Masala typically features chicken tikka (marinated and grilled chicken pieces) in a spicier, often tangier, tomato-based sauce that can include more onion and bell peppers.
*   **Can I make Butter Chicken ahead of time?** Absolutely! The flavors of Butter Chicken often deepen and improve overnight, making it an excellent dish for meal prep or entertaining.
*   **Can I freeze Butter Chicken?** Yes, it freezes well. If possible, add the fresh cream when reheating rather than freezing it with the cream already incorporated, as cream can sometimes separate upon thawing and reheating. Thaw overnight in the refrigerator and reheat gently on the stovetop.

Making classic Butter Chicken is a culinary journey that rewards you with one of the most comforting and flavorful dishes in the world. Enjoy the process, savor the aromas, and delight in the rich taste of your homemade Murgh Makhani!
